summary: PR #2746 made a one-hunk decomp matching improvement in `src/melee/gr/grpushon.c` for `grPushOn_802190D0`, changing a light-type assert from `HSD_ASSERT(698, HSD_LObjGetType(lobj)==LOBJ_POINT)` to `HSD_ASSERTMSG` with an explicit flags/type-mask condition while preserving the original assert text string.

key_files.role: Only changed file; contains `grPushOn_802190D0` assert source-shape adjustment.
evidence_refs: raw_diff.diff: hunk in grPushOn_802190D0 around line 425

what_changed.change: Reported objdiff score improved from about 98.35% to 99.19%; PR body states final status was 99.19481% with 2 arg mismatches and 1 replace.

smart_moves.description: Kept the original assert expression text as the `HSD_ASSERTMSG` message while changing the compiled condition shape to direct `lobj->flags` masking.
Limited the matching tactic to a narrow one-line assert condition rewrite instead of broad function churn.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For assert expressions involving HSD light object type checks, the source shape that matches may be an `HSD_ASSERTMSG` preserving the original text while the condition itself uses direct typed field access such as `(lobj->flags & LOBJ_TYPE_MASK) == LOBJ_POINT`.

Preserving assert text can matter independently from the expression used to generate code; here the PR explicitly kept the old `HSD_LObjGetType(lobj)==LOBJ_POINT` text while altering the executable condition.

naming_conventions.convention: No new naming convention was established; existing names such as `grPushOn_802190D0`, `lobj`, `LOBJ_TYPE_MASK`, `LOBJ_POINT`, and `LOBJ_DIFFUSE` were retained.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use direct flag masking for the runtime light-type condition while retaining the original human-readable assert expression as the `HSD_ASSERTMSG` string.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.reported_result: `objdiff-cli`: `grPushOn_802190D0` now 99.19481% with 2 arg mismatches and 1 replace; bot report shows 98.35% before and 99.19% after.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Verify the single-object build target and objdiff after the assert-shape change.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.reported_result: `ninja build/GALE01/src/melee/gr/grpushon.o` and `objdiff-cli` were listed in the PR body.

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR number, title, author, state, URL, timestamps, changed file counts
notes: No review comments or human reviewer feedback were present in the loaded slice.
The PR body and bot report provide verification claims, but this intake did not independently run build or objdiff.
The evidence supports a narrow lesson about this assert-shape tactic; it does not prove a universal rule that all `HSD_LObjGetType` asserts should be rewritten this way.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Preserve assert text while changing assert condition source shape when matching supports it
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2746, `grPushOn_802190D0` improved by replacing an `HSD_ASSERT` call using `HSD_LObjGetType(lobj)==LOBJ_POINT` with `HSD_ASSERTMSG` whose condition directly masked `lobj->flags` with `LOBJ_TYPE_MASK`, while preserving the original `HSD_LObjGetType(lobj)==LOBJ_POINT` text as the message.

source_update_candidates.title: grpushon light-type assert matching detail
source_update_candidates.text: For `src/melee/gr/grpushon.c`, PR #2746 indicates `grPushOn_802190D0` matched better when the light-type assertion used `HSD_ASSERTMSG(698, (lobj->flags & LOBJ_TYPE_MASK) == LOBJ_POINT, "HSD_LObjGetType(lobj)==LOBJ_POINT")` instead of calling `HSD_LObjGetType` in the condition.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is path- and function-specific historical matching evidence useful for future work in `grpushon.c`; it should not be promoted as a global HSD assert rule without more examples.

rejection_notes: Do not generalize this PR into a global requirement to replace `HSD_LObjGetType` calls with direct flag masking; the slice only shows one function-specific matching improvement.
Do not infer reviewer acceptance rationale beyond merge metadata; no review comments were loaded.
Do not treat the reported objdiff/build verification as independently revalidated by this intake.
